title: Which Marine Corps Leadership Principle Is Key To Successful Operations
source_filename: Which Marine Corps Leadership Principle Is Key To Successful Operations.docx

qas.q: We obtain our values through the influence of our ________, ________, and ________ with earlier experiences having more of an effect than later experiences.
qas.a: Parents, teachers, and peers

qas.q: Attitudes are not as deeply held as values.
qas.a: True

qas.q: CATEGORIES
qas.a:
Values are organized into the following five categories:
•Political values
•Social values
•Personal values
•Economic values
•Religious values

qas.q: CHARACTER
qas.a: Our character is defined by "...the commitment to an admirable set of values, and the courage to manifest those values in one's life, no matter the cost in terms of personal success or popularity." (Sorely, 1989)

qas.q: VALUES AND LEADERSHIP
qas.a:
If a Marine is left without any guidance or supervision, then personal values will determine what that Marine will or will not do.
Leaders must provide guidance and supervision to inspire and reinforce organizational values to have an effect on the behavior of our Marines.

qas.q: IDENTIFYING WITH PEERS VICE UNIT
qas.a: When there is a breakdown in vertical cohesion within the parent command, Marines only identify with their peers and not with their unit. Marines in a group or section are only loyal to those Marines within the immediate group.

qas.q: IMPACT ON UNIT SUCCESS
qas.a: Some Marines fail to make the connection between group objectives and command mission. Failing to understand the impact they have on the operation of the unit causes a disassociation from the unit and the Marine Corps.

qas.q: INSUFFICIENT CHALLENGES
qas.a: Many Marines joined the Corps to be challenged to the fullest extent of their capabilities. Disillusionment occurs if their units have low individual expectations.

qas.q: INEFFECTIVE MENTORS
qas.a: When new Marines check into a unit, they are looking to others to confirm accepted behaviors. Without a proper mentor, a Marine may choose a negative role model simply because of a dominant personality.

qas.q: LACK OF MARINE CORPS STANDARDS
qas.a: A witnessed deviation from, and a lack of adherence to, required standards leaves a negative opinion of both the command and the leadership. If Marines are continually exposed to such an environment, their standards will begin to suffer as their expectations and sense of accomplishment dwindle.

qas.q: INSUFFICIENT DISCIPLINE
qas.a: Discipline is degraded when commands relax standards, whether traditional customs or published regulations. All Marines are responsible for and capable of enforcing good discipline.

qas.q: CONFLICT RESOLUTION
qas.a: The best and most important way to resolve conflicting values is through leadership by example.

qas.q: A Marine's sense of pride in who they are stems from a dedication to our ________.
qas.a: Organizational values

qas.q: As a leader, it is your job to sustain organizational values, set the example, and guide the way for new Marines.
qas.a: true

qas.q: As much as anything, our ________ set us apart.
qas.a: Core values

qas.q: Marine Corps core values increase our readiness by providing a ________.
qas.a: Foundation for teams to function more effectively

qas.q: Honor includes personal qualities of ________.
qas.a: Dedication, trust, and dependability

qas.q: MENTAL COURAGE
qas.a: Just before the Battle of Thermoplyae, a Spartan warrior named Dienekes was told that the Persian archers could blank out the sun with their arrows. He replied "Good, then we shall have our battle in the shade." Spartans were mentally prepared for war from a young age.

qas.q: MORAL COURAGE
qas.a: In 1968, CWO Hugh Thompson was flying a support mission over My Lai when he realized something was terribly wrong. U.S. soldiers were slaughtering hundreds of unarmed civilians. With great moral courage Thompson landed his helicopter between the U.S. soldiers and the villagers and ordered his crew to train their weapons on the U.S. troops. He rescued 16 civilians calling in additional helicopter support to evacuate them. He would later report the incident to his chain of command.

qas.q: PHYSICAL COURAGE
qas.a: In early 2004 while serving on the Iraqi border with Syria, Jason Dunham's platoon was checking vehicles in conjunction with an attack on their PSD element. One of the drivers became combative, and Jason was engaged in hand-to-hand combat when the driver dropped an armed grenade in the midst of the Marines. Jason covered the grenade with his own helmet and used himself to shield his platoon members. This great physical courage saved the lives of those closest to him.

qas.q: TRAINING AND COURAGE
qas.a: Courage is not a chance gift of nature. It is developed through hard realistic training and unit cohesion that brings about a special bond amongst its members.

qas.q: Courage is the uncompromising code of integrity to respect human dignity and value others.
qas.a: false

qas.q: What is the importance of our core values of honor, courage, and commitment?
qas.a: They give strength, influence attitudes, and regulate behaviors.

qas.q: The bedrock of a Marine's character is _______.
qas.a: Honor

qas.q: The Marine Corps philosophy of leadership is characterized by ________.
qas.a:
T


he belief that leadership qualities can be developed within the individual Marine

qas.q: Which of the following are desired leadership qualities established by the Marine Corps philosophy of leadership? Select all that apply
qas.a:
Inspirational example
Technical proficiency
Moral responsibility

qas.q: The objective of Marine Corps leadership is to develop ________.
qas.a: Leadership qualities of Marines, enabling them to assume progressively greater responsibilities in the Marine Corps and in society

qas.q: The four most common styles of leadership are ________.
qas.a: Telling, selling, participating, and delegating

qas.q: In the ________ style of leadership, the leader may discuss alternate solutions, but ultimately makes the decision themselves.
qas.a: Participating

qas.q: The one common thread among great leaders is the ability to read people and select the leadership style that best communicates orders.
qas.a: True

qas.q: Which leadership trait is achieved by weighing the pros and cons accordingly to arrive at an appropriate decision?
qas.a: Judgment

qas.q: ________ permits a senior to assign a task with the understanding that it will be accomplished with minimum supervision.
qas.a: Dependability

qas.q: The leadership trait ________ involves impeccable personal appearance.
qas.a: bearing

qas.q: KNOW YOURSELF AND SEEK SELF-IMPROVEMENT
qas.a: Evaluate yourself by using the leadership traits to determine your strengths and weaknesses. Then simply utilize your strengths and improve your weaknesses.

qas.q: BE TECHNICALLY AND TACTICALLY PROFICIENT
qas.a: As a Marine, you must demonstrate your ability to accomplish the mission, and to do this you must be capable of answering questions and demonstrating competence in your MOS as well as in the profession of arms.

qas.q: DEVELOP A SENSE OF RESPONSIBILITY AMONG SUBORDINATES
qas.a: Assigning tasks and delegating the authority to accomplish tasks promotes mutual confidence and respect between the leader and subordinates. It also encourages initiative and cooperation in the accomplishment of unit tasks.

qas.q: MAKE SOUND AND TIMELY DECISIONS
qas.a: Hesitation or a reluctance to make a decision leads subordinates to lose confidence in your abilities as a leader.

qas.q: SET THE EXAMPLE
qas.a: As a leader of Marines, your duty is to set the standard by personal example.

qas.q: KNOW YOUR MARINES AND LOOK OUT FOR THEIR WELFARE
qas.a: Knowledge of your Marines' personalities will enable you, as the leader, to decide how to best handle each Marine and determine when more supervision is needed.

qas.q: KEEP YOUR MARINES INFORMED
qas.a: Informing your Marines of the situation makes them feel that they are a part of the team and not just a cog in the wheel.

qas.q: SEEK AND TAKE RESPONSIBILITY FOR YOUR ACTIONS
qas.a: Seeking responsibilities also means that you take responsibility for your actions.

qas.q: ENSURE ASSIGNED TASKS ARE UNDERSTOOD, SUPERVISED, AND ACCOMPLISHED
qas.a: Supervision is essential! Without supervision you cannot know if the assigned task is being properly accomplished.

qas.q: TRAIN YOUR MARINES AS A TEAM
qas.a: Personal qualities, leadership abilities

qas.q: EMPLOY YOUR COMMAND IN ACCORDANCE WITH ITS CAPABILITIES
qas.a: Keep your Marines informed.

qas.q: Leadership traits develop ________, and leadership principles develop ________.
qas.a: Be technically and tactically proficient.

qas.q: Which leadership principle promotes efficiency and morale by making Marines feel that they are part of the team?
qas.a: Be visible and approachable.,Enforce good hygiene and sanitation.,Correct grievances and remove discontent.

qas.q: Which leadership principle can be developed by service schools, preparing yourself for the job requirements of the next higher rank, and independent research?
qas.a: Unselfishness

qas.q: Which of the following are methods to develop the Marine Corps leadership principle to know your Marines and look out for their welfare?
qas.a: Common thread among great leaders

qas.a: The Marine Corps leadership philosophy

ucmj

•Be visible. Be approachable.
•Get to know and understand each of your Marines.
•Give them confidence that you will ensure they are prepared for battle.
•Concern yourself with their living conditions.

•Enforce good hygiene and sanitation.
•Keep in touch with your Marines' thoughts.
•Ensure fair and equal awards distribution.
•Encourage individual development.
•Provide sufficient recreational time, and participate.
•Share hardships.

•Treating them fairly and firmly
•Providing tough, realistic training to ensure success and survival
•Thrusting goals into their lives to improve themselves and our Corps

You must ingrain in them a deep pride to keep their honor clean and an unconquerable, aggressive spirit.
